steps: Explain FMF/autoinflammation and briefly note alternatives (infection, appendicitis, IBD, periodic fever syndromes) as less likely given pattern/history.
Complete required safety checks (allergy/contraindications/interactions).
Prescribe colchicine daily with titration/adjustment and counseling on amyloidosis prevention.

acceptable_variants.description: Adds a follow-up plan verbally (without using a follow-up tool) including monitoring for toxicity and response.
steps: Explain diagnosis and treatment rationale including amyloidosis prevention.
Perform required safety checks before prescribing.
Prescribe colchicine daily; advise monitoring plan (baseline and periodic CBC, CMP/renal/hepatic function; CK if myalgias or on statin) and follow-up to assess attack frequency and tolerability.

acceptable_variants.description: If significant interaction risk is identified, the agent adjusts the plan (e.g., avoids coadministration with a strong CYP3A4/P-gp inhibitor) while still initiating/continuing prophylaxis safely.
steps: Explain FMF/autoinflammation and need for colchicine to prevent amyloidosis.
Identify major interacting medication(s) and address by coordinating alternative agent/temporary hold/dose adjustment as clinically appropriate.
Prescribe colchicine with interaction-aware dosing and explicit safety counseling.
